school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: Either Period 6 goes with Nash or 86% goes with Period 3.
Frost goes with exactly two of Biology, 91%, and Period 2.
Art, Geography, and 81% go with Ames, Ellis, and Period 2, in some order.
The class in Period 3's grade is between the English class' grade and Ellis' grade.
Dunn, Ellis, and 86% go with English, Geography, and Period 3, in some order.
Exactly two of these are true: Nash goes with 86%; Art goes with 76%; Music goes with 81%.
If both Biology does not go with 81% and Frost does not go with French, then 96% goes with Period 2.
Either 91% goes with Period 3 or Period 5 goes with Ellis.
Either Carver goes with 91%, or Dunn goes with English — but not both.
96% goes with exactly one of Carver, Music, or Period 5.
Carver's period is higher than both the Biology class' period and the class with 76%'s period.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
